Index.meta_title: Strix — Runtime governance for AI agents
Index.meta_description: Strix intercepts high-risk agent actions before they execute, issues proof receipts for every decision, and gives your team a policy surface instead of another dashboard. Try the live demo.

Hero.follow_twitter: Live demo · runs in your browser
Hero.title: Stop agents from <important>doing the wrong thing</important> — the first time, not the tenth.
Hero.description: Strix is runtime governance for AI agents. Every capability is policy-evaluated before it executes. Every decision emits a proof receipt. Agents get fast paths where they're safe, and hard walls where they're not.

Features.section_title: Policy at the capability surface — not bolted on after the fact.
Features.section_description: Strix sits between your agent and the world. Every tool call is matched to a capability, every capability carries risk metadata, and every decision is logged with a receipt you can audit, share, or pipe into your SIEM.
Features.feature1_description: Agents don't get access to APIs — they get capabilities with explicit risk levels and blast-radius metadata. Scope them per principal, per environment.
Features.feature2_description: Declarative policies written in code, not wedged into a system prompt. Block destructive actions outright. Require human approval on high-impact calls. Auto-allow routine work.
Features.feature3_description: Every decision emits a cryptographically-chained receipt: decision, policy, principal, arguments, estimated impact. Shareable, auditable, indexable.
Features.feature4_description: Wrap any tool call in a governed() adapter. TypeScript and Python SDKs. Works with OpenAI, Anthropic, LangChain, your own harness.
Features.feature5_description: Automatic escalation on approval_required decisions. Approvers see the exact action, policy reason, and impact estimate. No mystery queue.
Features.feature6_description: Hash-chained receipts give you tamper-evident history. Pipe to S3, Datadog, or your warehouse. Answer 'what did the agent try?' with one query.

Pricing.section_title: Start with the demo. Pay when you put it in front of real traffic.
Pricing.section_description: The demo is free forever. Production is usage-based on governed-call volume.

PricingPlan.premium_plan_description: For teams shipping agents to prod

FAQ.question: How is this different from a system prompt that says 'be careful'?
FAQ.answer: System prompts are suggestions. Strix is runtime enforcement. When an agent tries to refund $42k, a prompt might advise against it. Strix blocks the tool call before it reaches your payment processor, writes a receipt, and escalates. The difference is: one relies on the model's cooperation, the other doesn't.

CTA.title: Ready to see an agent try — and fail — to drain a shop?
CTA.description: The demo takes 30 seconds. Pick a scenario, hit run, watch Strix intercept. Then book a walkthrough for your stack.
